title: How do I configure fst traces for maximum performance?
body:
I've been outputting .vcd files and converting them to .fst using vcd2fst in gtkwave.

However, I thought it would be a good idea to output .fst files directly from Verilator.

The problem is that vcd2fst is substantially faster than generating .fst files directly from Verilator. This is with Verilator from Ubuntu 21.10, so Verilator 4.038.

In my case, Verilator simulation and output .vcd file 25s, vcd2fst 25s, launching gtkwave on .vcd file 44s.

The fastest way to open gtkwave is to generate .vcd file, then launch gtkwave with -o that runs vcd2fst first.
